title: Login Monitoring & Security Incident Procedures ‚Äî Brute Force to Clinical SSO

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security ¬ß164.308(a)(6) ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security ¬ß164.308(a)(6) (Security incident procedures) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.10.7: Login Monitoring & Security Incident Procedures ‚Äî Brute Force to Clinical SSO.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.7' running on sourcetype WinEventLog:Security and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
spl:
index=ad sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode="4625" earliest=-24h
| eval user=TargetUserName
| eval src=src_ip
| stats count values(EventCode) as codes dc(ComputerName) as dc_used by user, src
| where count>=15 AND dc_used>=3
| lookup hipaa_clinical_apps.csv src OUTPUT app_name
| sort - count
description: Surfaces credential stuffing and password spray against Hyperspace/SSO endpoints so incidents are detected and handled under HIPAA security incident procedures.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ad, citrix, okta and sourcetypes OktaIM2:log`, WinEventLog:Security, WinEventLog:Security`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Baseline legitimate auth patterns during go-live; (2) integrate with ES notable or SOAR playbooks; (3) document IR steps for suspected credential compromise affecting ePHI. Indexes required: `index=ad`, `index=citrix`, `index=okta`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log``, `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security``; Lookups: `hipaa_clinical_apps`, `hipaa_clinical_apps.csv`.
visualization: Choropleth (src geo if iplocation enabled), Table (user, src, count), Single value (distinct locked accounts).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ad` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4625, `index=okta` `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log` (if used), `index=citrix` AAA failures.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Baseline legitimate auth patterns during go-live; (2) integrate with ES notable or SOAR playbooks; (3) document IR steps for suspected credential compromise affecting ePHI.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ad sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode="4625" earliest=-24h
| eval user=TargetUserName
| eval src=src_ip
| stats count values(EventCode) as codes dc(ComputerName) as dc_used by user, src
| where count>=15 AND dc_used>=3
| lookup hipaa_clinical_apps.csv src OUTPUT app_name
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Login Monitoring & Security Incident Procedures ‚Äî Brute Force to Clinical SSO (¬ß164.308(a)(6))** ‚Äî Surfaces credential stuffing and password spray against Hyperspace/SSO endpoints so incidents are detected and handled under HIPAA security incident procedures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ad` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4625, `index=okta` `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log` (if used), `index=citrix` AAA failures. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ad; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ad, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **user** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **src** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count>=15 AND dc_used>=3` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Login Monitoring & Security Incident Procedures ‚Äî Brute Force to Clinical SSO (¬ß164.308(a)(6))** ‚Äî Surfaces credential stuffing and password spray against Hyperspace/SSO endpoints so incidents are detected and handled under HIPAA security incident procedures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ad` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4625, `index=okta` `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log` (if used), `index=citrix` AAA failures. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Choropleth (src geo if iplocation enabled), Table (user, src, count), Single value (distinct locked accounts).
grandmaExplanation: We surface credential stuffing and password spray against Hyperspace/SSO endpoints so incidents are detected and handled under HIPAA security incident procedures. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
